description_long: This is a rustic, bulbous vase with a wide, rounded body tapering to a narrow neck and flared rim. The surface is matte and uneven, with visible textural irregularities and a hand-crafted appearance, suggesting it may be made from fired clay or terracotta. A single, thick handle is attached from the upper neck to the shoulder, providing a functional grip. The coloration is a deep reddish-brown with subtle variations and mottling, indicative of natural earthenware. The base is slightly raised and rounded, allowing the vase to stand upright on flat surfaces. There are no visible decorative motifs or embellishments, giving it a simple, utilitarian charm.
description: A rustic, reddish-brown clay vase with a bulbous body, narrow neck, and a single thick handle. The surface is matte, textured, and unadorned, emphasizing its hand-crafted, utilitarian style.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the wide, flared rim and the opening of the vase, with the handle partially visible on the side.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the curvature of the neck and body, as well as the attachment of the handle to the upper neck.
description_view_2: Profile view displaying the full handle, the rounded body, and the slightly raised base, emphasizing the vase's silhouette.
description_view_3: Rear angled view focusing on the back of the vase, with the handle and the body’s curvature most prominent.
